---
title: "LLM-immigration-model-checks"
author: "Andrea Nasuto"
date: "2025-04-01"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
# load necessary libraries
library(caret)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(readxl)
```

```{r}
# define base directories
setwd("/Users/Andrea/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-TheUniversityofLiverpool/PhD/code/immigration-llm")

# change this path to 'test_dataset_translation' to use the translated test dataset ('test_dataset')
base_path_test <- "model_performance/data/test_dataset_raw"

# change this path to 'train_dataset_translation' to use the translated dataset ('train_dataset')
base_path_train <- "model_performance/data/train_dataset_raw"

output_dir <- "model_performance/output"

# load the data with translation from the excel
path_translation_test <- "model_performance/data/test_dataset_translation"

path_translation_train <- "model_performance/data/train_dataset_translation"
```

```{r}
### DEFINE WHICH TYPE OF ANALYSIS TO PRODUCE
# Defines which model type to include:
model_type <- "4bit" #8bit

# set to TRUE if translated datasets are considered
translation = FALSE

# set to TRUE if translation check is needed
translation_check = FALSE

# set dataset:
dataset <- "Test"

# set language filter (TRUE to exclude all except English, Spanish, and Multilanguage)
filter_languages <- FALSE 
```

```{r}
language_map <- list(
  # Arabic
  "mig-ar" = "Arabic",
  "mig-ar-L3" = "Arabic",
  "mig-ar-L3-extra" = "Arabic",
  "ar" = "Arabic",

  # Spanish
  "mig-es" = "Spanish",
  "mig-es-L3" = "Spanish",
  "es" = "Spanish",

  # Portuguese
  "mig-pt" = "Portuguese",
  "mig-pt-L3" = "Portuguese",
  "mig-pt-L3-extra" = "Portuguese",
  "pt" = "Portuguese",

  # French
  "mig-fr" = "French",
  "mig-fr-L3" = "French",
  "mig-fr-L3-extra" = "French",
  "fr" = "French",

  # Italian
  "mig-it" = "Italian",
  "mig-it-L3" = "Italian",
  "mig-it-L3-extra" = "Italian",
  "it" = "Italian",

  # German
  "mig-de" = "German",
  "mig-de-L3" = "German",
  "de" = "German",

  # Hindi
  "mig-hi" = "Hindi",
  "mig-hi-L3" = "Hindi",
  "hi" = "Hindi",

  # Hungarian
  "mig-hu" = "Hungarian",
  "mig-hu-L3" = "Hungarian",
  "hu" = "Hungarian",

  # Indonesian
  "mig-in" = "Indonesian",
  "mig-in-L3" = "Indonesian",
  "in" = "Indonesian",

  # Turkish
  "mig-tr" = "Turkish",
  "mig-tr-L3" = "Turkish",
  "mig-tr-L3-extra" = "Turkish",
  "tr" = "Turkish",

  # Polish
  "mig-pl" = "Polish",
  "mig-pl-L3" = "Polish",
  "pl" = "Polish",

  # Korean
  "mig-ko" = "Korean",
  "mig-ko-L3-extra" = "Korean",
  "ko" = "Korean"
)
```

```{r}
extract_model_dataset <- function(filename) {
  filename_clean <- gsub(".csv", "", filename)
  parts <- unlist(strsplit(filename_clean, "_"))
  
  # extract model name
  model_end <- max(grep("llama", parts))
  model <- paste(parts[1:model_end], collapse = "-")
  
  # add translation flag if present in filename
  if (any(grepl("translation", parts))) {
    model <- paste0(model, "-translation")
  }
  
  dataset_start <- min(grep("mig-", parts))
  dataset_code <- parts[dataset_start]
  dataset <- ifelse(dataset_code %in% names(language_map), language_map[[dataset_code]], dataset_code)
  # read the file to extract tweet_lang values
  #df <- read.csv(filename)
  
  # assume tweet_lang is uniform or use the first non-missing value
  #lang_code <- as.character(df$tweet_lang[which(!is.na(df$tweet_lang))[1]])
  
  # translate to dataset using language_map
  #dataset <- ifelse(lang_code %in% names(language_map),
  #                  language_map[[lang_code]],
  #                  lang_code)
  
  return(data.frame(Model = model, Dataset = dataset, stringsAsFactors = FALSE))
}
```

```{r}
# Function to enrich CSV file with 'translation_checked' column from corresponding Excel file
enrich_with_translation_checked <- function(original_file, excel_path) {
  base_name <- basename(original_file)
  excel_file <- file.path(excel_path, sub("\\.csv$", "_checked.xlsx", base_name))
  
  original_data <- tryCatch(read.csv(original_file), error = function(e) return(NULL))
  if (is.null(original_data)) return(NULL)
  
  if (!file.exists(excel_file)) return(original_data)
  
  excel_data <- tryCatch(read_excel(excel_file), error = function(e) return(NULL))
  if (is.null(excel_data)) return(original_data)
  
  if ("translation_checked" %in% colnames(excel_data) && nrow(original_data) == nrow(excel_data)) {
    original_data$translation_checked <- excel_data$translation_checked
  }
  
  return(original_data)
}
```

```{r}
process_conf_matrix <- function(file_path) {
  message("Processing: ", file_path)
  
  data <- tryCatch(
    read.csv(file_path),
    error = function(e) {
      message("Failed to read CSV at: ", file_path)
      message("Error: ", e$message)
      return(NULL)
    }
  )
  
  if (is.null(data)) return(NULL)

  # Check if required columns exist
  if (!all(c("label", "label_llama") %in% colnames(data))) {
    message("Missing required columns in: ", file_path)
    message("Found columns: ", paste(colnames(data), collapse = ", "))
    return(NULL)
  }

  # Check if data is not empty
  if (nrow(data) == 0) {
    message("Empty dataset at: ", file_path)
    return(NULL)
  }

  # Convert to factors
  data$label <- factor(data$label)
  data$label_llama <- factor(data$label_llama, levels = levels(data$label))

  # Safely compute confusion matrix
  cm <- tryCatch(
    error = function(e) {
      message("Error in confusionMatrix for: ", file_path)
      message("Error: ", e$message)
      return(NULL)
    }
  )
  
  if (is.null(cm)) return(NULL)

  acc_CI <- c(cm$overall["AccuracyLower"], cm$overall["AccuracyUpper"])

  dataset_info <- extract_model_dataset(basename(file_path))
  return(data.frame(
    Model = dataset_info$Model,
    Dataset = dataset_info$Dataset, 
    Accuracy_Lower = acc_CI[1], 
    Accuracy_Upper = acc_CI[2], 
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  ))
}
```

```{r}
# load data based on dataset filter
accuracy_data <- data.frame()

# For the "Test" dataset
if (dataset == "Test" || dataset == "both") {
  # List files in base_path_test, excluding those that contain "_translation"
  file_list_test <- list.files(path = base_path_test, pattern = "^llama-32-3B-.*\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)
  file_list_test <- file_list_test[!grepl("_translation", file_list_test)]  # Exclude files containing "_translation"
  
  accuracy_data_test <- do.call(rbind, lapply(file_list_test, process_conf_matrix))
  accuracy_data <- rbind(accuracy_data, accuracy_data_test)
}

# For the "Train" dataset
if (dataset == "Train" || dataset == "both") {
  # List files in base_path_train, excluding those that contain "_translation"
  file_list_train <- list.files(path = base_path_train, pattern = "^llama-32-3B-.*\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)
  file_list_train <- file_list_train[!grepl("_translation", file_list_train)]  # Exclude files containing "_translation"
  
  accuracy_data_train <- do.call(rbind, lapply(file_list_train, process_conf_matrix))
  accuracy_data <- rbind(accuracy_data, accuracy_data_train)
}
```

```{r}
# apply model type filter
if (model_type == "F16") {
  accuracy_data <- accuracy_data %>% filter(!grepl("Q4", Model))
} else if (model_type == "4bit") {
  accuracy_data <- accuracy_data %>% filter(grepl("Q4", Model))
}

# Remove rows where 'dataset' contains "mig-multi-translation-L3" or 'model' contains "full"
accuracy_data <- accuracy_data %>%
  filter(!grepl("mig-multi-translation-L3", Dataset)) %>%
  filter(!grepl("full", Model))

accuracy_data <- as.data.frame(accuracy_data, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# ensure unique values for pivot_wider
accuracy_data <- accuracy_data %>%
  group_by(Model, Dataset) %>%
  summarise(Accuracy_Lower = mean(Accuracy_Lower, na.rm = TRUE),
            Accuracy_Upper = mean(Accuracy_Upper, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")
```

```{r}
# rename models after filtering
model_map <- function(model) {
  if (grepl("llama-32-3B-en-Q4-translation", model)) {
    return("English-Translation")
  } else if (grepl("llama-32-3B-en-es", model)) {
    return("English-Spanish")
  } else if (grepl("llama-32-3B-multi", model)) {
    return("Multilanguage")
  } else if (grepl("llama-32-3B-es", model)) {
    return("Spanish")
  } else if (grepl("llama-32-3B-en", model)) {
    return("English")
  } else {
    return(model)
  }
}

accuracy_data$Model <- sapply(accuracy_data$Model, model_map)
```

```{r}
# apply language filter
if (filter_languages) {
  allowed_languages <- c("English", "Spanish", "Multi", "Eng-Spa")
  accuracy_data <- accuracy_data %>% filter(Dataset %in% allowed_languages)
}
```

```{r}
# create wide-format table
accuracy_table <- accuracy_data %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Dataset, values_from = c(Accuracy_Lower, Accuracy_Upper))

# exclude specific datasets
excluded_datasets <- c("English", "Eng-Spa", "Spanish Translation", "Multi")
accuracy_data <- accuracy_data %>% filter(!Dataset %in% excluded_datasets)
```

### Add translation check

```{r}
# Clean extractor for language from *_checked.xlsx filenames
extract_language_from_checked <- function(filename) {
  filename <- gsub("_checked.xlsx", "", filename)
  parts <- unlist(strsplit(filename, "_"))
  
  dataset_code <- parts[grep("mig-", parts)]
  if (length(dataset_code) == 0) return(NA)
  
  language <- full_language_map[[dataset_code]]
  if (is.null(language)) return(NA)
  
  return(language)
}
```

```{r}
# Determine which translation_checked path to use
if (translation_check) {
  if (dataset == "Test") {
    translation_checked_path <- path_translation_checked_test
  } else if (dataset == "Train") {
    translation_checked_path <- path_translation_checked_train
  } else {
    stop("translation_check is TRUE but dataset is not 'Test' or 'Train'")
  }

  # List all *_checked.xlsx and *_checked.csv files in the selected path
  checked_files <- list.files(
    translation_checked_path,
    pattern = "_checked\\.(xlsx|csv)$",
    full.names = TRUE,
    ignore.case = TRUE
  )
}
```

```{r, eval=FALSE}
translation_avgs <- list()

# Loop through each checked file and extract the average of translation_check
for (file in checked_files) {
  df <- tryCatch(readxl::read_excel(file), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(df)) next
  if (!"translation_check" %in% names(df)) next
  
  # get the language (e.g. "Italian")
  language <- extract_language_from_checked(basename(file))
  if (is.null(language) || is.na(language)) next
  
  avg_value <- mean(df$translation_check, na.rm = TRUE)
  translation_avgs[[language]] <- avg_value
}
```

```{r}
# remove datasets not present in the checked translation list of datasets
if (translation_check) {
  # Filter accuracy_data to only include languages in translation_avgs
  accuracy_data <- accuracy_data %>%
    filter(Dataset %in% names(translation_avgs))
  
  # Add Translation_Avg column by matching language
  accuracy_data$Translation_Avg <- sapply(accuracy_data$Dataset, function(lang) translation_avgs[[lang]])
}
```

### Visualization
```{r}
# dynamic title for the chart
chart_title <- paste("Accuracy Confidence Intervals for", dataset, "Dataset")

# function to create accuracy confidence interval plots
plot_accuracy_intervals <- function(data, title) {
  ggplot(data, aes(x = Dataset, ymin = Accuracy_Lower, ymax = Accuracy_Upper, color = Model, fill = Model)) +
    geom_errorbar(width = 0.3, position = position_dodge(width = 0.6)) +
    geom_point(aes(y = (Accuracy_Lower + Accuracy_Upper) / 2), size = 3, position = position_dodge(width = 0.6)) +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) ifelse(x %in% names(language_map), language_map[[x]], x)) +
    labs(title = title,
         x = "Language",
         y = "Accuracy Confidence Interval",
         color = "Model",
         fill = "Model") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom",
          panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
          plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
          legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
          legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
          axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
}

# create and display the plot
plot <- plot_accuracy_intervals(accuracy_data, chart_title)
print(plot)
```
```{r}
# save results
filter_status <- ifelse(filter_languages, "main_languages", "all_languages")
output_filename <- paste0("accuracy_", dataset, "_", model_type, "_", filter_status, ifelse(translation, "_translation", ""), ".csv")
output_path_csv <- file.path(output_dir, output_filename)

output_png <- gsub(".csv", ".png", output_path_csv)
ggsave(output_png, plot, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

cat("Processing complete. Results saved to:", output_path_csv, "\n")
```

```{r}
# run this code if translation = TRUE

if (translation_check) {
  # set chart title with translation quality
  chart_title_translation <- paste("Accuracy Confidence Intervals for", dataset, "Dataset with Translation Quality")
  
   plot_accuracy_intervals_translation <- function(data, title) {
    ggplot(data, aes(x = Dataset,
                     ymin = Accuracy_Lower,
                     ymax = Accuracy_Upper,
                     color = Model,
                     fill = Model,
                     alpha = Translation_Avg)) +  # 👈 Add alpha here
      geom_errorbar(width = 0.3, position = position_dodge(width = 0.6)) +
      geom_point(aes(y = (Accuracy_Lower + Accuracy_Upper) / 2,
                     size = Translation_Avg),   # 👈 Add size here
                 position = position_dodge(width = 0.6)) +
      scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) ifelse(x %in% names(language_map), language_map[[x]], x)) +
      scale_size_continuous(range = c(2, 6)) +  # Adjust for visibility
      scale_alpha_continuous(range = c(0.4, 1)) +
      labs(title = title,
           x = "Language",
           y = "Accuracy Confidence Interval",
           color = "Model",
           fill = "Model",
           size = "Translation Quality",
           alpha = "Translation Quality") +  # 👈 Label legends
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(
        legend.position = "bottom",
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
        plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 8, face = "bold"),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 5),                 
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
      )
  }
  
  # create and display the plot
  plot_translation <- plot_accuracy_intervals_translation(accuracy_data, chart_title_translation)
  print(plot_translation)
  
  # save the dataset in with ggsave
  #ggsave(paste0("accuracy_plot_translation_quality_", dataset, ".png"), plot_translation, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)
}
```
